root_text.an4.101:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an4.101:2.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cattāro valāhakūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.101:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā hoti no vassitā?
root_text.an4.101:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo bhāsitā hoti, no kattā.
root_text.an4.101:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā hoti, no vassitā.
root_text.an4.101:3.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako gajjitā, no vassitā;
root_text.an4.101:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo vassitā hoti, no gajjitā?
root_text.an4.101:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo kattā hoti, no bhāsitā.
root_text.an4.101:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo vassitā hoti, no gajjitā.
root_text.an4.101:4.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako vassitā, no gajjitā;
root_text.an4.101:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo neva gajjitā hoti, no vassitā?
root_text.an4.101:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo neva bhāsitā hoti, no kattā.
root_text.an4.101:5.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo neva gajjitā hoti, no vassitā.
root_text.an4.101:5.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako neva gajjitā, no vassitā;
root_text.an4.101:6.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā ca hoti vassitā ca?
root_text.an4.101:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo bhāsitā ca hoti kattā ca.
root_text.an4.101:6.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā ca hoti vassitā ca.
root_text.an4.101:6.4: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako gajjitā ca vassitā ca;
root_text.an4.101:6.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro valāhakūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.101:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an4.101:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an4.101:2.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of clouds.
translation_text.an4.101:2.5: one neither thunders nor rains, and
translation_text.an4.101:2.7: These are the four kinds of clouds.
translation_text.an4.101:2.8: In the same way, these four individuals similar to clouds are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.101:2.12: one neither thunders nor rains, and
translation_text.an4.101:3.1: And how does an individual thunder but not rain?
translation_text.an4.101:3.2: It’s when an individual is a talker, not a doer.
translation_text.an4.101:3.3: That’s how an individual thunders but doesn’t rain.
translation_text.an4.101:3.4: That individual is like a cloud that thunders but doesn’t rain, I say.
translation_text.an4.101:4.1: And how does an individual rain but not thunder?
translation_text.an4.101:4.2: It’s when an individual is a doer, not a talker. …
translation_text.an4.101:5.1: And how does an individual neither thunder nor rain?
translation_text.an4.101:5.2: It’s when an individual is neither a talker nor a doer. …
translation_text.an4.101:6.1: And how does an individual both thunder and rain?
translation_text.an4.101:6.2: It’s when an individual is both a talker and a doer. …
translation_text.an4.101:6.6: These four individuals similar to clouds are found in the world.”

variant_text.an4.101:6.4: valāhako gajjitā ca → gajjitā ca hoti (pts1ed, mr)
